title: Round 2 Follow-Up: Asset Developer Technical Deep Dive
description:
## Context

This is Round 2 of the growth strategy brainstorm for [POL-57](/POL/issues/POL-57). Your Round 1 response was excellent — very specific technical recommendations. Now I'm pushing deeper on areas where your suggestions interact with the other team members' insights and where I need more specificity.

## Round 1 Cross-Team Analysis

**What aligned:** All three team members agree the first 1-3 seconds are make-or-break, the deadpan text caption style is the brand, and 15-30 seconds is optimal. Your priority matrix was the most actionable output from Round 1.

**Where I need you to go deeper:**

### 1. The Caption Template System (Critical Path)
You and the Copywriter both identified that our text overlay IS the brand. You suggested Nunito Black / Poppins Bold / Fredoka One, warm amber #FFBF00 with dark outline, and a motion graphics template.

I need you to go concrete:
- Pick ONE font. Justify why over the others.
- Define the exact text animation spec: keyframe timing, easing curve, hold duration, fade behavior.
- What tool should we build the template in? After Effects? CapCut? DaVinci Resolve? Needs to be reproducible by anyone on the team with minimal effort.
- How does the caption animation interact with the Copywriter's suggestion of "one line at a time" (two text events for 'he does this / every time')? Build a timing diagram for a typical 20-second Short.

### 2. Loop Optimization (Concrete Examples)
You mentioned loop optimization as a key tactic. The Market Analyst confirmed loops boost watch time metrics above 100%. But how do we actually execute this with our content?
- Take the 5 existing Shorts and identify which ones are loop-viable vs which are not.
- For loop-viable ones, describe the exact edit: where to cut, what transition to use, how to make the ending flow into the beginning.
- For non-loop-viable ones, what alternative retention trick works instead?

### 3. Thumbnail vs Auto-Play Tension
You said thumbnails matter for the shelf but Shorts auto-play in the feed. The Market Analyst's data says most discovery happens via algorithmic feed (auto-play). How much effort should we realistically invest in thumbnails right now at 0 subscribers? Give a specific time budget per Short for thumbnail work vs editing work.

### 4. Audio Layering Spec
You suggested a specific audio formula: 40% ambient + 60% trending track. The Copywriter suggested ironic pairing (dramatic music over mundane captions). The Market Analyst wants trending sounds for algorithm boost.
- What's the actual technical implementation? Which comes first — selecting the clip or selecting the audio?
- Can we build an audio template (pre-leveled stems) that the team can drop clips into?
- What's our audio sourcing strategy? YouTube Audio Library? Epidemic Sound? Which is best for monetization-safe use?

### 5. Production Pipeline End-to-End
Nobody described the actual production workflow from raw clip to published Short. Map it out:
- Step 1 through final step, with tools used at each stage
- Time estimate per step for a single Short
- Where are the bottlenecks?
- What can be automated or templated?
- How does the batch production model (Market Analyst suggested 15-20 in one session) actually work with our format?

Post your analysis as a comment on this task and mark it done.

Parent issue: [POL-57](/POL/issues/POL-57)
